antenna-base.name: Antenna Base
antenna-base.description:
You emerge from the tunnel into a vast open space. The tower rises above -- a skeletal lattice of steel stretching hundreds of meters into hazy sky. {pink:Red warning lights} still blink at its peak. The wind hums through the lattice like a struck tuning fork.

Your {purple:implant} buzzes constantly now. A {purple:camp} has been set up to the west -- tents, a fire pit, banners with a strange symbol. The {pink:Conduit} is here.

examine.tower: A massive broadcast antenna, built before the Collapse. The structure is sound -- ATLAS built to last. The blinking {pink:red lights} mean it's still powered and operational.
examine.banners: Cloth banners with a symbol: a circle with radiating lines -- like a broadcast signal. The Conduit's icon. They worship ATLAS as a sleeping god.
examine.camp: Maybe 20 people live here, judging by the tents. Devoted followers who believe the signal is a prophecy.

conduit-camp.name: Conduit Camp
conduit-camp.description:
Tents arranged in a circle around a central shrine: a {purple:server rack} draped in cloth and surrounded by candles. It would be absurd if it weren't so earnest. Incense and ozone mingle in the air.

A {purple:robed figure} approaches. Their expression is welcoming, but their eyes are intense.

examine.shrine: A working server rack powered by a jury-rigged solar panel. Status lights blink in the same rhythm as the signal. Infrastructure turned into iconography.
examine.tents: Simple but well-maintained. Books, tools, and technical manuals stacked neatly inside each one. They're not just worshippers -- they're students of the old technology.
examine.candles: Candles arranged in a pattern around the shrine. You recognize it -- a {purple:neural interface} circuit diagram. These people know more than they let on.

choices.text: Head back to the antenna base

relay-room.name: Relay Room
relay-room.description:
The antenna's control room. Banks of {cyan:relay equipment} line the walls, most still operational. Screens display signal strength, broadcast patterns, and routing data.

One screen catches your eye: a map showing a single line connecting this antenna to a point labeled {pink:ATLAS CORE} -- deep underground, to the southwest.

equipment.text: Broadcast relay equipment. The signal originates from ATLAS Core underground, gets amplified here, and broadcasts across the old network. The power output is enormous -- this signal was meant to reach {bright:everywhere}.

map.text:
The tunnel to ATLAS Core starts beneath the antenna, descends through maintenance shafts, and reaches the core facility about 2 kilometers underground. The route is marked: {dim:MAINTENANCE ACCESS -- CLEARANCE OMEGA REQUIRED}.

Omega clearance. That's what your personnel file said you had.

examine.screens: Signal strength, packet routing, error rates. All nominal. ATLAS's automation kept this running perfectly for 40 years.

choices.text: Check the map to ATLAS Core

antenna-tower.name: Antenna Tower Platform
antenna-tower.description:
A maintenance platform halfway up the tower. Wind howls around you, tugging at your clothes. The entire region spreads below: Dusthaven to the east, The Spine crossing the wasteland, Sector 7-G directly beneath.

To the southwest, a massive depression in the earth -- a sinkhole or excavation site. That must be where the tunnel leads. {bright:ATLAS Core.}

examine.core: The depression is enormous -- maybe a kilometer across. Not natural. Something was built there, or something collapsed. The tunnel leads directly to it.
examine.landscape: The full scope of the post-Collapse world. Ruined cities, overgrown farmland, the dark line of The Spine. And everywhere, the signal, pulsing through dead infrastructure like a ghost in the machine.

choices.text: Listen to the signal from up here

descent-entrance.name: Descent Entrance

examine.door: A blast door rated for nuclear attack. Two meters thick. Someone with serious clearance -- or serious tools -- opened this.
examine.shaft: The elevator shaft drops into blackness. The ladders look solid, but it's a long way down.
examine.preparation: You check your supplies and take stock. Whatever is down there, you'll need everything you've got.

choices.text: Begin the descent into the shaft
